 {"id":"bd-b6xo","title":"Remove or fix ClearDirtyIssues() - race condition risk (bd-52)","description":"Code health review found internal/storage/sqlite/dirty.go still exposes old ClearDirtyIssues() method (lines 103-108) which clears ALL dirty issues without checking what was actually exported.

Data loss risk: If export fails after some issues written to JSONL but before ClearDirtyIssues called, changes to remaining dirty issues will be lost.

The safer ClearDirtyIssuesByID() (lines 113-132) exists and clears only exported issues.

Fix: Either remove old method or mark it deprecated and ensure no code paths use it.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:20.534625-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b6xo","depends_on_id":"bd-tggf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:19:05.633738-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

-{"id":"bd-hmeb","title":"bd slot set doesn't support cross-beads prefix routing","description":"## Problem

`bd slot set` fails when setting a slot to a bead that lives in a different beads database (cross-db routing).

## Evidence

When spawning a polecat:
```
Warning: could not set role slot: bd slot set gt-gastown-polecat-chumbucket role hq-polecat-role: 
Error: failed to resolve bead hq-polecat-role: no issue found matching "hq-polecat-role"
```

The agent bead `gt-gastown-polecat-chumbucket` is in rig beads (`gastown/mayor/rig/.beads/`).
The role bead `hq-polecat-role` is in town beads (`~/gt/.beads/`).

`bd show hq-polecat-role` works fine (uses prefix routing via routes.jsonl).
`bd slot set ... hq-polecat-role` fails (does not use routing).

## Root Cause

`bd slot set` resolves the target bead in the current database only, ignoring the `hq-` prefix that should route to town beads.

## Expected Behavior

`bd slot set <agent> <slot> <bead>` should:
1. Check if `<bead>` has a routable prefix (e.g., `hq-`, `gt-`, `bd-`)
2. If so, resolve it via routes.jsonl like `bd show` does
3. Store the bead reference (cross-db references are valid for slots)

## Workaround

Currently the warning is non-fatal - the role slot just is not set. But this breaks role-based queries.

## Files

- `cmd/slot.go` - slot set command implementation
- Need to add routing logic similar to `cmd/show.go`","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/polecats/garnet","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:12:40.230597-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:13:33.548834-08:00"}

The agent bead `gt-gastown-polecat-chumbucket` is in rig beads (`gastown/mayor/rig/.beads/`).
The role bead `hq-polecat-role` is in town beads (`~/gt/.beads/`).

`bd show hq-polecat-role` works fine (uses prefix routing via routes.jsonl).
`bd slot set ... hq-polecat-role` fails (does not use routing).

## Root Cause

`bd slot set` resolves the target bead in the current database only, ignoring the `hq-` prefix that should route to town beads.

## Expected Behavior

`bd slot set <agent> <slot> <bead>` should:
1. Check if `<bead>` has a routable prefix (e.g., `hq-`, `gt-`, `bd-`)
2. If so, resolve it via routes.jsonl like `bd show` does
3. Store the bead reference (cross-db references are valid for slots)

## Workaround

Currently the warning is non-fatal - the role slot just is not set. But this breaks role-based queries.

## Files

- `cmd/slot.go` - slot set command implementation
- Need to add routing logic similar to `cmd/show.go`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/polecats/garnet","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:12:40.230597-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:19.493134-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:19.493134-08:00","close_reason":"Added prefix routing to bd slot set command.  {"id":"bd-hnkg","title":"GH#540: Add silent quick-capture mode (bd q)","description":"Add bd q alias for quick capture that outputs only issue ID. Useful for piping/scripting. See GitHub issue #540.","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:03:38.260135-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"feature"}
 {"id":"bd-hobo","title":"Distinct prefixes for protos, molecules, wisps","description":"Template/workflow entities should have visually distinct prefixes from regular issues.

**Problem:**
- Protos (bd-7bs4) look like regular issues - invites squashing
- Molecules (poured instances) also use bd- prefix
- Wisps are in separate DB but still use bd- when referenced

**Proposed Prefixes:**
- `proto-` for templates (e.g., proto-release, proto-review)
- `mol-` for active molecules (poured from protos)
- `wisp-` for ephemeral wisps (vapor phase)

**Benefits:**
- Instant visual recognition of entity type
- Prevents accidental modification of templates
- Clear lifecycle: proto → mol → wisp → digest

**Implementation options:**
1. Separate databases with different prefixes
2. Issue type determines prefix generation
3. Naming convention enforced by bd pour/wisp commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-24T16:45:24.940809-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T02:04:52.459233-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T02:04:52.459233-08:00","labels":["workflow"]}
 {"id":"bd-hp8g","title":"gt sling should look up formulas from .beads/formulas/ registry","description":"## Problem

gt sling <formula-name> doesn't find formulas from the registry that bd formula list shows.

## What I tried

1. gt sling beads/crew/emma --formula beads-release --var version=0.42.0
   - Problem: --formula flag doesn't exist

2. gt sling beads-release beads/crew/emma --var version=0.42.0
   - Error: open .../beads-release: no such file or directory
   - Problem: Looks for file in cwd, not formula registry

## What worked

bd mol wisp beads-release --var version=0.42.0 - correctly finds formula from .beads/formulas/

## Expected

gt sling should use the same formula lookup as bd mol wisp - checking .beads/formulas/*.formula.json

## Documentation gap

The help shows examples like 'gt sling mol-release mayor/' suggesting formula slinging works, but it doesn't find registry formulas.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/polecats/jade","created_at":"2025-12-30T21:18:17.251292-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:06:27.153176-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T22:06:27.153176-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed in both repos: bd cook now looks up formulas from registry, and gt sling uses bd mol wisp"}

 {"id":"bd-umbf","title":"Design contributor namespace isolation for beads pollution prevention","description":"## Problem

When contributors work on beads-the-project using beads-the-tool, their personal work-tracking issues leak into PRs. The .beads/issues.jsonl is intentionally tracked (it's the project's issue database), but contributors' local issues pollute the diff.

This is a recursion problem unique to self-hosting projects.

## Possible Solutions to Explore

1. **Contributor namespaces** - Each contributor gets a private prefix (e.g., `bd-steve-xxxx`) that's gitignored or filtered
2. **Separate database** - Contributors use BEADS_DIR pointing elsewhere for personal tracking
3. **Issue ownership/visibility flags** - Mark issues as "local-only" vs "project"
4. **Prefix-based filtering** - Configure which prefixes are committed vs ignored

## Design Considerations

- Should be zero-friction for contributors (no manual setup)
- Must not break existing workflows
- Needs to work with sync/collaboration features
- Consider: what if a "personal" issue graduates to "project" issue?

## Expansion Needed

This is a placeholder. Needs detailed design exploration before implementation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:00:29.638743-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T18:12:30.976419-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T17:05:13.732967-08:00","close_reason":"Design complete. See docs/CONTRIBUTOR_NAMESPACE_ISOLATION.md. Recommends auto-routing approach with role detection. Key gap identified: bd-6x6g needs implementation to actually route issues to target repo."}

 {"id":"bd-usro","title":"Rename 'template instantiate' to 'mol bond'","description":"Rename the template instantiation command to match molecule metaphor.

Current: bd template instantiate <id> --var key=value
Target:  bd mol bond <id> --var key=value

Changes needed:
- Add 'mol' command group (or extend existing)
- Add 'bond' subcommand that wraps template instantiate logic
- Keep 'template instantiate' as deprecated alias for backward compat
- Update help text and docs to use molecule terminology

The 'bond' verb captures:
1. Chemistry metaphor (molecules bond to form structures)
2. Dependency linking (child issues bonded in a DAG)
3. Short and active

See also: molecule execution model in Gas Town","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T16:56:37.582795-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T23:22:43.567337-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T23:22:43.567337-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-usvv","title":"Defer SQLite metadata updates until after git commit succeeds","description":"Part of GH#885 fix: bulletproof sync atomicity.

Currently exportToJSONL() immediately:
- Clears dirty flags in SQLite
- Updates last_import_time metadata

This happens BEFORE git commit, so if commit fails, SQLite "lies" about sync state.

Fix: Move metadata updates to AFTER git commit succeeds:
1. Export JSONL to disk (atomic file write)
2. Stage and commit to git
3. ONLY THEN clear dirty flags and update last_import_time

Key files:
- cmd/bd/sync_export.go:147-170 (premature updates)
- cmd/bd/sync.go:555 (commit point)

This is the core fix for the atomicity gap.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/crew/dave","created_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:23.619319-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:07:37.691736-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-usvv","depends_on_id":"bd-pnx2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:55.798447-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma"}]}
+{"id":"bd-usvv","title":"Defer SQLite metadata updates until after git commit succeeds","description":"Part of GH#885 fix: bulletproof sync atomicity.

Currently exportToJSONL() immediately:
- Clears dirty flags in SQLite
- Updates last_import_time metadata

This happens BEFORE git commit, so if commit fails, SQLite "lies" about sync state.

Fix: Move metadata updates to AFTER git commit succeeds:
1. Export JSONL to disk (atomic file write)
2. Stage and commit to git
3. ONLY THEN clear dirty flags and update last_import_time

Key files:
- cmd/bd/sync_export.go:147-170 (premature updates)
- cmd/bd/sync.go:555 (commit point)

This is the core fix for the atomicity gap.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/crew/dave","created_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:23.619319-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:21.245756-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:21.245756-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented deferred metadata updates - finalizeExport() now runs after git commit succeeds","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-usvv","depends_on_id":"bd-pnx2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:55.798447-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma"}]}
 {"id":"bd-uu8p","title":"Routing bypassed in daemon mode","description":"## Bug

Prefix-based routing (routes.jsonl) only works in direct mode. When the daemon is running, it bypasses routing logic because:

1. Daemon mode resolves IDs via RPC (`daemonClient.ResolveID`)
2. Daemon uses its own store, which only knows about local beads
3. The routing logic in `resolveAndGetIssueWithRouting` is only called in direct mode

## Reproduction

```bash
# With daemon running:
cd ~/gt
bd show gt-1py3y  # Fails - daemon can't find it

# With daemon bypassed:
bd --no-daemon show gt-1py3y  # Works - uses routing
```

## Fix Options

### Option A: Client-side routing detection (Recommended)
Before calling daemon RPC, check if the ID prefix matches a route to a different beads dir. If so, use direct mode with routing for that ID.

```go
// In show.go, before daemon mode:
if daemonClient != nil && needsRouting(id) {
    // Fall back to direct mode for this ID
    result, err := resolveAndGetIssueWithRouting(ctx, store, id)
    ...
}
```

### Option B: Daemon-side routing
Add routing support to the daemon RPC server. More complex - daemon would need to:
- Load routes.jsonl on startup
- Open connections to multiple databases
- Route requests based on ID prefix

### Option C: Hybrid
Daemon returns "not found + prefix hint", client retries with routing.

## Recommendation

Option A is simplest. The client already has routing logic; just use it when we detect a routed prefix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-26T14:52:00.452285-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T14:54:51.572289-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-26T14:54:51.572289-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-uutv","title":"Work on beads-rs0: Namepool configuration for themed pole...","description":"Work on beads-rs0: Namepool configuration for themed polecat names. See bd show beads-rs0 for full details.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T21:49:48.129778-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T21:59:25.565894-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T21:59:25.565894-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-uwkp","title":"Phase 2.4: Git merge driver optimization for TOON format","description":"Optimize git 3-way merge for TOON line-oriented format.

## Overview
TOON is line-oriented (unlike binary formats), enabling smarter git merge strategies. Implement custom merge driver to handle TOON-specific merge patterns.

## Required Work

### 2.4.1 TOON Merge Driver
- [ ] Create .git/info/attributes entry for *.toon files
- [ ] Implement custom merge driver script/command
- [ ] Handle tabular format row merges (line-based 3-way)
- [ ] Handle YAML-style format merges
- [ ] Conflict markers for unsolvable conflicts

### 2.4.2 Merge Patterns
- [ ] Row addition: both branches add different rows → union
- [ ] Row deletion: one branch deletes, other modifies → conflict (manual review)
- [ ] Row modification: concurrent field changes → intelligent merge or conflict
- [ ] Field ordering changes: ignore (TOON format resilient to order)

### 2.4.3 Testing & Documentation
- [ ] Unit tests for merge scenarios (3-way merge logic)
- [ ] Integration tests with actual git merges
- [ ] Conflict scenario testing
- [ ] Documentation of merge strategy

## Success Criteria
- Git merge handles TOON conflicts intelligently
- Fewer manual merge conflicts than JSONL
- Round-trip preserved through merges
- All 70+ tests still passing
- Git history stays clean (minimal conflict markers)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:14.339238776-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:26.434306-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:26.434306-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uwkp","depends_on_id":"bd-iic1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:14.34427988-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
